result:
Build is clean. Only pre-existing warnings remain. The fix is complete.

**Root cause:** `LuaState::gc_gen` was a `todo!("phase-b-reconcile: gc_gen")` stub in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/state_stub.rs:172` (in the `LuaStateStubExt` trait default). The test's `collectgarbage("generational")` call at gengc.lua:12 routed through `collectgarbage_fn` → `state.gc_gen(...)` → the unimplemented default, panicking. (The "stack overflow" tail you quoted is stale — the current failure is the `gc_gen` `todo!()`.) Sibling stubs `gc_inc` and `gc_set_param` had the same hole.

**Fix:** Wired the three GC trait methods to the already-implemented `lua_vm::api::gc(...)` via `GcArgs::Gen`, `GcArgs::Inc`, and `GcArgs::SetPause`/`SetStepMul` (selected by op code 6/7). Added the impls in the `impl LuaStateStubExt for LuaState` block alongside the existing `gc_count`/`gc_step`/`gc_is_running`/`gc_control_simple` overrides. No instrumentation was added, so nothing to remove.

**New state:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` succeeds with only pre-existing unrelated warnings. I did not re-run the test program per instructions; the panic site (`gc_gen`) is now reachable through the real `lua_vm::api::gc` path that other GC operations already use successfully.
